institution.name: Northwell Health - North Shore University Hospital
aliases: Zucker School of Medicine — Visiting Students (covering NSUH)
institution.healthSystem: Northwell Health / Donald and Barbara Zucker SOM at Hofstra-Northwell

searchTermsTried: Hofstra Northwell School of Medicine visiting medical student VSLO international North Shore University Hospital

candidateFindings.pageTitle: Visiting Students - Zucker School of Medicine
candidateFindings.shortQuote: All students attending US LCME accredited medical schools or COCA accredited AACOM member colleges are required to use the AAMC Visiting Student Learning Opportunities service (VSLO).
candidateFindings.specialtyDecision: Multispecialty across 23+ Northwell hospital sites including NSUH

candidateFindings.pageTitle: Visiting Students - Zucker School of Medicine
candidateFindings.shortQuote: Due to the volume of applications, we do not accept applicants from foreign medical schools that do not already have an affiliation agreement with the Zucker School of Medicine.

negativeEvidence.strongNegativeEvidence: Verbatim exclusion: foreign medical schools without pre-existing Zucker affiliation are NOT accepted. International lane is affiliation-only, not open public pathway.
negativeEvidence.weakNegativeEvidence: NSUH not separately named verbatim as one of the 23+ sites; placement is by department in VSLO.

nextAction: For public-safe activation: (a) US LCME/COCA via VSLO; (b) no Zucker fees (only AAMC VSLO standard fee); (c) max 12 weeks; (d) foreign MS = pre-affiliation-only — not open international.
plainEnglishSummary: Northwell North Shore University Hospital is one of 23+ Northwell sites accessible through Zucker School of Medicine at Hofstra-Northwell's visiting students program. US LCME or COCA-accredited schools only via VSLO. No Zucker-specific fees (only AAMC's standard VSLO fee). Max 12 weeks. International medical schools can apply only if they already have an affiliation agreement with Zucker — not an open international pathway.
driftCheck: This advanced national USCE discovery because NY gained a second P101 packet (after Bellevue); affiliation-only international caveat captured verbatim.
